title: Debugger and final project deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Debugger and final project; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Debugger and final project: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A project is complete once it runs from the developer's source directory and produces the expected output for one sample.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

items.title: Debugger and final project: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Python: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'fresh environment'. Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
items.template: Verify the built artifact from a [[checkpoint]] and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.

answer.explanation: Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.
